title: Lontar
content:
{{Top|fga|leg|notoc=1}} {{Species |image= |name=Lontar |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]][[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions= |lifespan= |origin=[[Lontar system/Legends|Lontar system]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|I am from an old clan, an even older nation, swallowed by the Republic, taken in after our defeat at the hands of the Lontars.''"
"''That was hundreds of years ago. The senate forced the Lontars to stop their aggression.|The Blood Carver [[Ke Daiv]] discusses his history with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]|''[[Rogue Planet]]''}} The '''Lontars''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Lontar system/Legends|Lontar system]] of the north-western portion of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies Region]]. Several hundred [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Declaration of a New Order|end]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Lontars [[Battle of Batorine|attacked]] [[Batorine]], a neighboring [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the Colonies and the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Blood Carver]]s, a feudal, reclusive species that was extremely adept at [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]]. The Lontars defeated the Blood Carvers so completely that their target species was driven almost to the point of [[Speciecide|extinction]]; the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], however, intervened in the hostilities and forced the Lontars to halt their onslaught. Although some Blood Carvers remained on Batorine following the Lontars' cessation of hostilities, most of the survivors were taken to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the Republic, where they were kept in seclusion, finally joining the Republic in [[110 BBY]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Lontars were created by [[Greg Bear]] for his novel ''[[Rogue Planet]]'', published by [[Del Rey]] in [[2000]]. They later received a mention in [[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']], written by [[Ann Margaret Lewis (real person)|Ann Margaret Lewis]] and published in [[2001]]. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', published in [[2009]], provided the location of the Lontar home [[Star system/Legends|star system]], placing it in the same grid as Batorine. The timing of the Lontars' assault on the Blood Carvers is somewhat unclear. In ''Rogue Planet'', [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] describes the conflict as occurring "hundreds of years ago," and the Blood Carver [[Ke Daiv]] describes his people as being "swallowed by the Republic, taken in after [their] defeat." In ''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'', however, the Blood Carvers are said to join the Republic only in 110 BBY, a mere 81 years before Skywalker's conversation. ==Appearances== *''[[Rogue Planet]]'' {{1stm|in book}} ==Sources== *[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] {{C|Under ''Blood Carver''}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Colonies sentient species]]
